quick_skim.title: Dual Sparse Aggregation Transformer for Multispectral Object Detection
quick_skim.abstract: Transformer-based approaches have obtained excellent performance in multispectral object detection tasks due to their ability to model long-range dependencies and capture complementary information. However, previous transformer-based multispectral detection methods tend to use all available tokens for similarity calculation, which results in redundant information interaction from irrelevant areas, leading to degraded detection performance. To overcome this challenge, we propose a novel Dual Sparse Aggregation Transformer (DSAFormer) for multispectral object detection, which consists of a Dual Sparse Transformer (DSFormer) and a Learnable Addition Fusion Block (LAFB). Specifically, the DSFormer is designed to exploit and boost cross-modal complementary information, thereby improving detection performance. It incorporates three key components: A Spatial Sparse Multi-Head Cross-Attention (SSMHCA) mechanism selectively captures cross-modal relationships at the spatial level by reserving only the high query-key similarity scores, eliminating irrelevant interactions. A Channel Sparse Multi-Head Cross-Attention (CSMHCA) mechanism performs similar sparse calculations at the channel level to enhance feature representation and filter out low matching query-key. A Multi-Scale Feature Refinement Layer (MSFRL) is developed to aggregate hierarchical features and suppress redundant information. To effectively fuse multimodal features, the LAFB is introduced to aggregate intramodal and intermodal feature information by feature reweighting. Extensive experimental results have demonstrated that our proposed DSAFormer achieves better detection performance against state-of-the-art methods on four public datasets, including the MFAD, FLIR, M$^3$FD, and LLVIP. The source code of our DSAFormer will be released at https://github.com/WenCongWu/DSAFormer.
quick_skim.llm_evidence_en: multispectral fusion for object detection, applicable to remote sensing multi-sensor fusion
quick_skim.llm_evidence_cn: 多光谱融合的目标检测方法，可用于遥感多传感器融合
quick_skim.llm_evidence: 多光谱融合的目标检测方法，可用于遥感多传感器融合
quick_skim.canonical_evidence: 多光谱融合的目标检测方法，可用于遥感多传感器融合
quick_skim.llm_tldr_en: Proposes DSAFormer for multispectral object detection, using Dual Sparse Transformer and Learnable Addition Fusion Block to reduce redundant cross-modal interaction and improve fusion efficiency.
quick_skim.llm_tldr_cn: 现有基于Transformer的多光谱目标检测方法在使用所有token进行相似度计算时，会导致来自无关区域的冗余信息交互，降低检测性能。为此，本文提出DSAFormer，由双稀疏Transformer（DSFormer）和可加性融合模块（LAFB）组成，旨在挖掘并增强跨模态互补信息，同时抑制冗余交互。实验表明，该方法在多个多光谱检测基准上取得了优异性能，为多模态遥感数据融合提供了高效方案。
quick_skim.llm_tldr: 现有基于Transformer的多光谱目标检测方法在使用所有token进行相似度计算时，会导致来自无关区域的冗余信息交互，降低检测性能。为此，本文提出DSAFormer，由双稀疏Transformer（DSFormer）和可加性融合模块（LAFB）组成，旨在挖掘并增强跨模态互补信息，同时抑制冗余交互。实验表明，该方法在多个多光谱检测基准上取得了优异性能，为多模态遥感数据融合提供了高效方案。
quick_skim.matched_query_text: methods for fusing multimodal remote sensing data from different sensors

quick_skim.title: AnyMatch: Supercharging Universal Multi-Modal Image Matching with Large-Scale Single-View Images
quick_skim.abstract: Multi-modal image matching is essential for visual localization and multi-sensor fusion, but it is hindered by the scarcity of large-scale training data with precise geometric annotations. Existing real-world datasets suffer from prohibitive costs, limited scene diversity, and errors in SfM-MVS pipelines, while synthetic methods struggle to maintain 3D geometric consistency or achieve photorealistic appearance. To address this, we propose AnyMatch, a novel framework that leverages abundant, easily accessible single-view images at minimal cost to generate rich multi-modal training data. AnyMatch integrates monocular depth estimation, 3D reprojection, diffusion-based inpainting, and crossmodal image translation to synthesize multi-view, multi-modal image pairs with 3D geometric fidelity. Crucially, our method provides annotations that strictly adhere to 3D geometric consistency through explicit 3D reprojection, avoiding SfM-MVS error accumulation. Furthermore, AnyMatch offers strong scalability, enabling controllable scene diversity and annotation difficulty via adjustable input and camera parameters. We construct Any-syn, a large-scale synthetic multi-modal dataset using AnyMatch. Experimental results show that matching networks (e.g., LoFTR, EDM, RoMa) fine-tuned on Any-syn achieve substantial performance gains on multi-modal benchmarks, exhibiting superior generalization and robustness compared to models trained on existing data.
quick_skim.llm_evidence_en: universal multi-modal image matching for multi-sensor fusion, applicable to remote sensing
quick_skim.llm_evidence_cn: 通用多模态图像匹配用于多传感器融合，可应用于遥感
quick_skim.llm_evidence: 通用多模态图像匹配用于多传感器融合，可应用于遥感
quick_skim.canonical_evidence: 通用多模态图像匹配用于多传感器融合，可应用于遥感
quick_skim.llm_tldr_en: Proposes AnyMatch to generate multi-modal training data from single-view images via depth estimation and cross-modal translation, boosting multi-modal image matching performance.
quick_skim.llm_tldr_cn: 多模态图像匹配受限于大规模几何标注训练数据的稀缺，现有数据集成本高且多样性有限。本文提出AnyMatch，利用单视图图像通过单目深度估计、3D重投影和跨模态翻译生成丰富的多模态训练数据。实验表明，该方法在多个匹配基准上显著提升性能，为遥感多传感器融合提供了低成本数据生成方案。
